title: chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. naktongense
intro: Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. naktongense, also known as Korean marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. naktongense include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Chrysanthemum zawadzkii subsp. naktongense is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in traditional herbal preparations, such as tinctures and ointments.
